note: behavioral half-bridge driver subckt: input logic threshold ~5 V (VCC-independent internal ref) -- a 3.3 V logic source will NOT switch it, level-shift to >=5 V (e.g. a 2N7000 inverter). Collapses under whole-circuit use_initial_condition; op-point start (no uic) converges. Subckt node order != KiCad symbol pin order -- map Sim_Pins by NAME (find_spice_model --symbol Driver_FET:IR2104)
trap: Behavioral high/low-side driver subckt with a model-specific, roughly VCC-independent ~5 V logic threshold. A 3.3 V logic drive never crosses it, so the output stays idle while the input toggles -- and a single-device find_spice_model --verify (load + op-point) passes anyway because it never checks the threshold. Also COLLAPSES under whole-circuit use_initial_condition (uic); a plain .op start (no uic) converges. Subckt node ORDER != KiCad symbol pin order.
detect: Op-point the driver in an ISOLATED harness and probe IN vs the datasheet logic threshold; sweep input amplitude to find where HO/LO start switching. If the whole circuit is run with uic it will fail to converge -- drop uic and start from .op.
workaround: Level-shift the logic input up to the threshold (a 2N7000 common-source inverter turns a 3.3 V swing into 0/12 V); raise VCC above UVLO; assert the enable/SD pin. Map Sim_Pins by NAME, not position (find_spice_model --symbol Driver_FET:IR2104); start transients from .op (no uic).

note: real 400 V subckt (loads; device-level boost converged to 213 V). Terminal identity is NOT tool-known -- verify D/G/S against the vendor header; the IR heuristic is 10=D 20=G 30=S
trap: Stiff vendor MOSFET subckt (POWMOS.LIB). Two distinct failures: (1) inside a high-gain (Aol~1e6) DC control loop the Newton solver has no basin at mid-range setpoints even though the extremes converge ('No convergence in dc analysis'); (2) under hard commutation its internal capacitances stall the integrator ('Timestep too small; trouble with xq1:dmos-instance'). Terminal identity is NOT tool-known.
detect: Op-point / .op converges at setpoint extremes but not mid-range; transient aborts on the switching edge with a dmos-instance timestep-too-small tail. Real 400 V part (device-level boost converged to 213 V).
workaround: UIC transient settle (seed loop nodes at 0, charge a ~10 nF cap ~20 ms, tail-average) instead of .op at the failing setpoints; RC snubber (~100 Ohm + 680 pF) + gate series R to tame dv/dt in switching runs. NEVER add a large conditioning resistor across the pass device -- it corrupts Vout. Verify D/G/S against the vendor header; the IR/Intusoft heuristic is 10=Drain 20=Gate 30=Source.

note: VDMOS: loads + conducts once terminals resolve by pin NAME (position-mapping came out gate/drain-swapped = a dead device); common-source .op V(DRN)=0.0014 V after the fix
trap: VDMOS power MOSFET: loads and conducts, BUT terminal identity must be resolved by pin NAME. A position-based Sim_Pins mapping came out gate/drain-swapped = a DEAD device (no conduction), which a load-only verify does not catch.
detect: Common-source .op reads V(DRN)=0 (dead) with a positional pin map; V(DRN)=0.0014 V once D/G/S are resolved by pin name.
workaround: Map Sim_Pins by NAME (find_spice_model --symbol ...); never position-map a 3-terminal power-FET subckt.

note: loads + single-device op-point converges, but numerically STIFF in multi-instance transient feedback loops (timestep collapse in the internal CMOS macromodel) -- prefer a bipolar-input part (e.g. LT1364) for oscillator/loop cores
trap: CMOS-input op-amp macromodel whose internal MOSFETs will not converge in a multi-instance transient feedback loop even with stiff=True + UIC -- the transient timestep collapses ('trouble with mosfet-instance m.x in op-amp macromodel'). A single-device op-point verify does not predict this; the collapse only appears once several instances close a loop.
detect: --verify LOADS and the op-point converges, but the full transient collapses once >1 instance is wired into a feedback loop.
workaround: Swap the oscillator/loop core to a bipolar-input model (LT1364-class); reduce the instance count; use bipolar supply rails so the model biases in-range.

note: known-good: loads + converges, robust in multi-instance transient loops (used as the FuncGen VCO/shaper op-amp)
trap: None known in these designs -- this is the bipolar-input part the CMOS-macromodel convergence failures were resolved by swapping TO. Converges in multi-instance transient feedback loops where CMOS-input macromodels (LMC6482-class) collapse.
workaround: Prefer for oscillator/loop cores and telemetry buffers; note it is high-GBW (~70 MHz) so still roll it off on unbypassed dividers next to switching nodes (see opamp-buffer-rails-switching).

note: loads from vendor_lib (used as the HV boost rectifier)
trap: None known -- ultrafast rectifier that loads from vendor_lib; used as the HV boost rectifier.
workaround: Use directly as an ultrafast rectifier.

note: FAILS-TO-LOAD in ngspice (verified 2026-07-12; only an unvetted hobbyist-lib model exists) -- pick a different comparator
trap: FAILS-TO-LOAD in ngspice (verified 2026-07-12): only an unvetted hobbyist-lib model exists in the corpus and it does not parse/load. A --verify smoke test reports FAILS-TO-LOAD.
workaround: Pick a different high-speed comparator that has a real, loadable model.
